headline: Procurement Risk Across Mexico's 6-Year Political Cycle
subtitle: 23 years of federal contracts reveal a structural pattern: the final year of every administration sees dramatically different procurement behavior — and it repeats across every president, every party.

keyFinding.label: Key Finding
keyFinding.body: Year 1 of a new administration sees the highest direct-award rates as incoming governments bypass competitive bidding to install preferred vendors quickly. Years 5–6 exhibit the highest risk scores — the "budget dump" effect as outgoing officials rush final spending. December contract spikes appear every year regardless of administration.

sexenio.heading: Sexenio Year Analysis
sexenio.description: How each year of the 6-year presidential term compares on procurement risk and procedure type — pooled across all administrations 2000–2024.

chart.legendNote: Peak bar highlighted. Year 1 = first year of administration.

sexenio.error: Sexenio year breakdown could not be loaded.

election.heading: Election Year Effect
election.description: Average procurement risk in federal election years vs non-election years (2002–2025). Mexican federal elections occur every 6 years for the presidency.
election.higherElection: Election years show measurably higher procurement risk, consistent with political spending pressure.
election.higherNonElection: Non-election years show higher procurement risk — election-year scrutiny may suppress fraud.
election.noDifference: No significant difference between election and non-election year risk.
election.error: Election year effect data could not be loaded.

admin.heading: Procurement by Administration
admin.description: Key procurement metrics by administration — Fox, Calderón, Peña Nieto, AMLO, Sheinbaum. Documented corruption cases and vendor concentration (HHI) by era.
admin.chartFootnote: GT = Ground truth cases in RUBLI database. Higher counts reflect both more corruption and more documented evidence — not necessarily a higher absolute rate.
admin.error: Administration comparison data could not be loaded.

methodology.label: Methodology:
methodology.body: This analysis groups 3,059,592 contracts (2002–2025) by the year within each 6-year presidential term (sexenio). Year 1 = first calendar year of a new president. Risk scores from RUBLI v0.6.5 model (AUC 0.828, vendor-stratified test split). High-risk threshold: score ≥ 0.40. Direct award = non-competitive procurement procedure. Single bid = competitive procedure with only one bidder. Election year = year of federal presidential election (2000, 2006, 2012, 2018, 2024). GT cases = contracts matched to documented corruption cases in the RUBLI ground truth database (748 cases as of 2026-03-25). Vendor concentration HHI is computed per era across all sectors.
